title: 1.1: Reports how kitty maintains window state during rapid creation and destruction (e.g., global_state structure, WITH_OS_WINDOW macros)
1.2: Identifies window creation mechanism (e.g., add_window function, ensure_space_for allocation, initialize_window call)
1.3: States resize signal propagation to child processes (e.g., pty_resize function, TIOCSWINSZ ioctl)
1.4: Reports signal handling callback, categorization mechanism (e.g., handle_signal function, SignalSet flags for kill_signal, child_died)
1.5: Identifies the complete deferred removal mechanism including flagging and queue processing (e.g., needs_removal flag, mark_child_for_close, remove_children function)
1.6: Reports resize debouncing mechanism (e.g., process_pending_resizes, resize_debounce_time threshold, on_pause comparison)
1.7: Identifies how poll mechanism affects signal timing (e.g., poll interruption, signal FD at children_fds[1])
1.8: States window destruction cascade (e.g., destroy_window function cleaning up pending_clicks, buffered_keys, GPU resources)
1.9: Reports close request state machine (e.g., close_request field states: no request, confirmable, confirmation in progress, imperative)
1.10: Reports test output, runtime observations demonstrating window lifecycle (e.g., creation/removal sequence, resize event handling)
